cap E { fn op(): A }
fn f(h: E): A { handle E with h in E.op }
---
f : (E) -> A
==========
cap E { fn op(): A }
fn g(): A / { E } { E.op }
fn f(h: E): A { handle E with h in g() }
---
g : () -> A / {E[E]}
f : (E) -> A
==========
cap E1 { fn op1(): A }
cap E2 { fn op2(): B }
fn f(h1: E1, h2: E2): A { handle E1 with h1 in handle E2 with h2 in E1.op1 }
---
f : (E1, E2) -> A
==========
cap E { fn op(): A }
fn f(a: A): A / {} { a }
---
f : (A) -> A
==========
cap E { fn op(): A }
fn inner(): A / { E } { E.op }
fn outer(): A / {} { inner() }
---
ERROR: requires cap `E`
==========
cap E { fn op(): A }
fn inner(): A / { E } { E.op }
fn outer(h: E): A { handle E with h in inner() }
---
inner : () -> A / {E[E]}
outer : (E) -> A
==========
cap E { fn op(): A }
fn f(): A / { .. } { E.op }
---
f : () -> A / {E[E]}
==========
cap E { fn op(): A }
cap E2 { fn op2(): B }
fn f(): A / { .., E2 } { E.op }
---
f : () -> A / {E[E], E2[E2]}
==========
cap E { fn op(): A }
fn g(): A { E.op }
fn f(h: E): A { handle E with h in g() }
---
g : () -> A / {E[E]}
f : (E) -> A
==========
cap E { fn op(): A }
fn g(): A { E.op }
fn f(): A { g() }
---
g : () -> A / {E[E]}
f : () -> A / {E[E]}
